newsSince: Showcased AI/data-center power-management solutions at PCIM Expo (June 1). Latest print (fiscal Q3 FY26) was a small GAAP net loss with revenue roughly flat; Q4 guide ~$168M assumes a return to sequential growth and margin expansion. Management is upbeat on advanced-computing/inference power but flagged limited H2 CY26 PC visibility from seasonality and memory shortages. No fresh catalyst since June 9.

sources.label: AOSL Q3 FY26 results / Q4 guide (StockTitan)

verdicts.rankRationale: On a node-bottleneck list AOSL is analog/power, indirect to HBM/CPO/packaging/test. Advanced-computing power tailwind is offset by H2 PC visibility caution and a Q3 net loss; no fresh evidence since prior case. Hold at zero on node centrality after a +122% YTD.
verdicts.bull: AI/data-center power demand and inference-driven higher power needs support content growth.
verdicts.neutral: Power semis are adjacent to, not central to, the AI-node bottleneck.
verdicts.bear: Q3 net loss, thin margins and weak H2 PC visibility cap the case after +122% YTD.
verdicts.primaryRisk: PC seasonality and memory-shortage demand drag

verdicts.rankRationale: On the test-time-scaling lens AOSL fits as power semis / power management for compute. Inference-driven higher power requirements are supportive, but the Q3 loss and limited H2 CY26 visibility balance it; no material change since prior case, so hold.
verdicts.bull: Inference/test-time scaling raises rack power, favoring power-management content.
verdicts.neutral: Power-management fit is real but a commoditized, cyclical slice of the scaling theme.
verdicts.bear: Margins under pressure and demand visibility limited into H2 CY26.
